key_themes: Explosive AI cloud infrastructure growth (84% YoY in Q3)
Record $553B RPO backlog providing multi-year revenue visibility
Stock rebound from 2026 lows as buying opportunity after 50%+ pullback from 2025 highs
Bullish analyst consensus with ~$244 average price target (significant upside from ~$175)
Heavy capex for AI capacity amid sustained demand outpacing supply
summary: Oracle (ORCL) sentiment is solidly positive as of mid-April 2026. The company delivered exceptional Q3 FY2026 results with cloud infrastructure revenue surging 84% and a massive $553 billion RPO backlog (heavily tied to AI deals including OpenAI), leading to raised fiscal 2027 guidance of $90 billion in revenue. While the stock fell sharply from its 2025 peak of ~$345 amid capex and debt concerns, it has rebounded to around $175 with YTD gains, and analysts/investors widely view the valuation as attractive for its transformation into a major AI computing and multicloud infrastructure player.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/why-now-time-buy-oracle-120500907.html)[[2]](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/10/oracle-orcl-q3-earnings-report-2026.html)[[3]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/ORCL)
sources: 15+ news articles (Yahoo Finance, Motley Fool, CNBC, Reuters, Bloomberg), 25+ recent X posts (Latest and semantic search since March 2026), Q3 FY2026 earnings report, guidance updates, and analyst ratings
